title: Carnage on Seve's Last Stand
theme: A day of widespread wreckage at Seve's final design: blow-ups for every player, Patterson's commanding Trophy lead shredded from ten to three, and Alex Baker quietly posting the round of the day to leap into second.
opening_hook: Open on the scoreboard reality: Patterson still leads the Trophy, but a ten-shot cushion has been hacked down to three after a round in which all six players posted at least one quadruple bogey or worse. Frame Royal Óbidos — Ballesteros' final design — as the venue that bit everyone.
foreshadow: Jon Baker bolts out of the gate with 16 points through five — pin that as the high-water mark before the wheels come off at 10–12
Patterson bleeds eight shots across holes 2–4 — early warning of the carnage that returns at the 12th and 18th
Alex Baker keeps his head down on the front while others detonate — setting up his round-of-the-day 42 points despite a 9 at the 14th

competition_state.name: Trophy
competition_state.note: Lead held but badly dented: a ten-shot cushion shrunk to three. Patterson's 34-point round was undone by twin blow-ups (8 at the 12th, 9 at the 18th), while Alex Baker's 42-point round vaulted him from joint third into clear second. Mullin slipped from 2nd to 3rd; Jon Baker stayed level with him on 110.

competition_state.name: Green Jacket
competition_state.note: Lead preserved at exactly the same 7-shot margin over Jon Baker — but only because Mullin's quintuple-bogey 9 at the 10th was matched, shot for shot, by Baker's own implosion at the 11th. Patterson remains 3rd at +20; Williams 4th. Alex Baker and Stuart Neumann are now tied at the back on 100.

competition_state.name: Wooden Spoon
competition_state.note: Neumann remains rooted to the foot of the Trophy table and the gap to Williams above him has actually widened from 14 to 15, despite a respectable 37-point return that included a tidy 12-point burst through 10–13. Two quadruple-bogey 8s (at the 4th and the 16th) and a closing 8-6-7 finish kept him pinned.

players.arc: Still the Trophy leader — but only just. Bled eight shots through 2–4, then served up a quadruple-bogey 8 at the 12th and a quadruple-bogey 9 at the 18th to see a 10-shot lead chopped to 3. A round of damage limitation that didn't quite limit the damage.

The day's quiet winner: 42 Stableford points, the best round of the day, including absorbing a quintuple-bogey 9 at the 14th without derailing. Climbed from joint third into clear second and is now the genuine Trophy threat with one round to play.
Holds the Green Jacket lead at exactly 7, but a quintuple-bogey 9 at the 10th and a modest 33-point Stableford return cost him second place in the Trophy race — slipping behind Alex Baker into third.

A round of two halves writ large: 16 points in the opening five holes, then a catastrophic 6-9-6 through 10–12 (his own quadruple bogey at the 11th) wiped out the cushion. Still second in the Jacket; still tied with Mullin in the Trophy.
Quietly accumulated 38 Stableford points — second-best of the day — despite a quadruple-bogey 7 at the par-3 15th. Gained ground on the field but remains 4th in both races.

37 points and flashes of life — 12 points through 10–13, 9 through 6–8 — but bookended by quadruple-bogey 8s at the 4th and 16th, and an 8-6-7 close. Still last, and the gap to Williams above him has actually grown.

venue_notes: Royal Óbidos — TEG's 3rd visit, Ballesteros' final course design, a Challenge Tour venue with dramatic water features. The carnage across all six cards (a 9, a 9, a 9, an 8, an 8, an 8, an 8, a 7…) speaks to the course's championship teeth. Worth a nod to Seve's fingerprints and the Lisbon Coast (TEG's third trip).
